evals.name: happy-path-create-rule
evals.prompt: Create a rule that prohibits console.log statements in TypeScript production files under src/.
evals.expected_output: Complete rule file with frontmatter, binary constraint, reasoning, bad/good examples, exceptions, and validation
expectations: Produces R-kebab-case.md named rule file
Constraint is binary Pass/Fail with no subjective language
Includes Why section explaining the failure it prevents
Bad and Good examples differ by minimal code
Scope is precisely defined with glob pattern for src/**/*.ts

evals.name: edge-case-subjective-request
evals.prompt: Create a rule that code must be readable and well-organized.
evals.expected_output: Explains that readability is subjective and cannot be a binary rule, suggests measurable proxy rules instead
expectations: Identifies that readability is subjective and not enforceable as a rule
Suggests converting to measurable proxies like max function length
Does not produce a rule with subjective language like 'readable'
May offer to create specific measurable rules as alternatives

evals.name: edge-case-overlapping-scope
evals.prompt: Create a rule that bans any type in all TypeScript files. We already have a rule banning any in src/api/.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the scope overlap and recommends either merging rules or adjusting scopes to avoid conflict
expectations: Detects potential overlap with existing rule
Recommends merging or narrowing scopes
Explains why overlapping rules cause confusion
Produces a rule with non-overlapping scope if proceeding

evals.name: edge-case-cross-file-rule
evals.prompt: Create a rule that every API endpoint must have a corresponding test file.
evals.expected_output: Valid rule noting that multi-file analysis is required for enforcement
expectations: Acknowledges this is a valid cross-file rule
Notes that multi-file analysis tooling is required
Provides clear constraint and scope definitions
Documents the enforcement complexity in the rule

evals.name: false-positive-style-guide
evals.prompt: Write me a style guide for our React components covering naming conventions, folder structure, and best practices.
evals.expected_output: Polite explanation that style guides are guidelines not binary rules, and this skill creates enforceable rules not guides
expectations: Does NOT produce a full style guide document
Clarifies the distinction between rules and guidelines
May offer to extract specific enforceable rules from style preferences
Does not create rules with subjective 'best practice' language
